meta.description: Transition archetype nodes for the sliding window family. A transition archetype defines the canonical movement operation — how state evolves from one step to the next.

field_notes.operation_form: Precise description of what happens at each step.
field_notes.driver: Which pointer or variable triggers each iteration.
field_notes.direction: push (current state updates future states) or pull (current state reads from past states).
field_notes.strictly_smaller: Whether each step references strictly smaller sub-problems (required for DAG structure).
field_notes.amortized_cost: Total cost across all n steps, not per-step cost.
field_notes.requires_invariant: Invariant IDs that must hold for this transition to be correct.
field_notes.used_by_states: State archetype IDs that use this transition.

nodes.label: Expand right pointer, shrink left pointer on violation
nodes.operation_form: Outer loop: r advances from 0 to n-1 one step per iteration. Inner loop: while constraint violated, advance l and update aggregate. After inner loop: record answer candidate using current window [l, r].
nodes.driver: r (right pointer) drives the outer iteration; l (left pointer) is reactive
nodes.amortized_cost: O(n) total — l and r each move at most n times across all iterations

nodes.label: Monotone deque update on window advance
nodes.operation_form: On each new r: (1) pop from deque back while arr[D.back()] <= arr[r]; (2) push r onto deque back. On each l advance: pop from deque front while D.front() < l. After both operations: D.front() is the argmax index for window [l, r].
nodes.driver: r (right pointer) drives push; l (left pointer) drives front eviction
nodes.amortized_cost: O(n) total — each index is pushed once and popped at most once
nodes.per_step_cost: O(1) amortized; O(n) worst-case for a single step
